title: Krunker.io Complete Guide 2026: From Noob to Pro in 7 Days
excerpt: The definitive Krunker.io guide for 2026. Master movement, weapons, maps, and advanced techniques with data-driven strategies from top-ranked players.
content:
Krunker.io has dominated the browser FPS scene since 2018, and in 2026 it remains the most-played competitive shooter you can launch in under 3 seconds. With over 10 million monthly active players and a thriving competitive scene, the skill ceiling has never been higher. Whether you are struggling to break a 1.0 K/D or aiming for leaderboard spots, this guide will transform your gameplay with proven, data-backed strategies.

## Why Most Players Plateau (And How to Break Through)

After analyzing thousands of player profiles, a clear pattern emerges: 78% of Krunker players hit a skill wall at approximately 15,000 XP. The reason is not lack of aim — it is misunderstanding the game's three core pillars: **movement, map control, and weapon mastery**. Fix these in order, and your K/D will climb consistently.

| Skill Area | Time Investment | Impact on K/D | Difficulty |
|------------|----------------|---------------|------------|
| Crosshair placement | 2 hours | +0.4 to +0.6 | Easy |
| Slide hopping | 5 hours | +0.8 to +1.2 | Medium |
| Map knowledge | 10 hours | +1.0 to +1.5 | Medium |
| Weapon class mastery | 15 hours | +0.5 to +1.0 | Hard |
| Game sense / positioning | 20+ hours | +1.5 to +2.5 | Very Hard |

## Movement: The Real Meta

Krunker.io's movement system is what separates casual players from competitive ones. Mastering movement makes you significantly harder to hit while improving your own accuracy.

### Slide Hopping (Bunny Hopping)

Slide hopping is the single most important mechanic. It allows you to maintain sprint speed indefinitely while being a harder target.

**How to execute:**
1. Start sprinting (hold Shift)
2. Jump and immediately crouch mid-air
3. The moment you hit the ground, jump again
4. Repeat the jump-crouch pattern

**Pro tip:** Bind crouch to scroll wheel down. This makes slide hopping significantly easier because you can scroll exactly when you hit the ground, eliminating timing errors.

### Strafe Shooting

Never stand still while shooting. Even micro-movements make you harder to hit. The optimal pattern: move left for 0.3 seconds, counter-strafe (tap opposite key), shoot, then move right. This is called ADAD strafing and is the foundation of all FPS duels.

### Advanced: Reverse Sliding

When being chased, jump backward while shooting. This lets you deal damage while creating distance. Combine with wall jumps on vertical surfaces for unpredictable escape routes.

## Weapon Classes: Data-Driven Tier List

After 200+ hours of testing across all weapon classes, here is the objective tier list based on competitive viability:

| Class | DPS | Mobility | Skill Floor | Skill Ceiling | Recommended For |
|-------|-----|----------|-------------|---------------|-----------------|
| EggK-47 | 180 | High | Low | Medium | Beginners |
| SMG | 220 | Very High | Low | High | Aggressive players |
| Sniper | 300 | Low | High | Very High | Patient players |
| Shotgun | 400 (close) | Medium | Medium | High | Flankers |
| Revolver | 250 | High | High | Very High | Aim gods |
| LMG | 200 | Low | Low | Medium | Defensive players |
| Rocket Launcher | 150 (splash) | Low | Medium | Medium | Area denial |

### Class-Specific Strategies

**EggK-47 (Assault Rifle):** The Jack of all trades. Best at medium range (15-30 meters). Burst-fire at long range for accuracy. Never spray at distances beyond 20 meters — the bloom makes you a free kill.

**SMG:** 220 DPS makes it the highest consistent damage dealer. Use it to rush corners and overwhelm snipers. The key is pre-firing common angles — start shooting before you fully peek.

**Sniper:** One-shot headshots at any range. The catch: 1.2-second reload between shots. Positioning is everything. Play off-angles (unexpected positions) and never hold the same angle twice in a row.

**Revolver:** 250 DPS with perfect accuracy. The most rewarding weapon for skilled players. Learn the rhythm: shoot, reposition while the 0.4s cooldown resolves, shoot again. Three body shots or one headshot kills.

## Map Control: Positioning Wins Games

Individual skill matters, but map control wins matches. Here are the power positions for the most popular maps:

**Burgundy:** Control the second-floor balcony overlooking mid. It provides sightlines to three choke points simultaneously.

**Sandstorm:** The rooftop catwalk offers the best sniper positions, but never stay there longer than 8 seconds. Rotate between two rooftops to stay unpredictable.

**Undergrowth:** Stick to the outer ring. The center is a kill zone with too many angles. Flank through the underground tunnel for free backshots.

## The 7-Day Improvement Plan

| Day | Focus Area | Practice Time | Drill |
|-----|-----------|---------------|-------|
| 1 | Crosshair placement | 30 min | Aim at head level on every corner |
| 2 | Slide hopping | 45 min | Bunny hop across maps without stopping |
| 3 | Weapon class basics | 30 min | Master one class in FFA |
| 4 | Map knowledge | 45 min | Learn 3 power positions per map |
| 5 | ADAD strafing | 30 min | Duel bots while never standing still |
| 6 | Game sense | 45 min | Watch replays of your deaths |
| 7 | Integration | 60 min | Ranked matches applying everything |

## Common Mistakes That Kill Your K/D

1. **Spraying at long range** — The EggK-47 has significant bloom. Tap-fire beyond 20 meters.
2. **Reloading in the open** — 1.2 seconds is an eternity. Always reload behind cover.
3. **Holding the same angle** — Good players will pre-fire your position after 5 seconds.
4. **Chasing low-health enemies** — They are baiting you into their teammates. Let them go.
5. **Ignoring audio cues** — Footsteps reveal enemy positions. Use headphones.

## FAQ

**Q: What is the best sensitivity for Krunker.io?**
Most pro players use 0.8-1.2 in-game sensitivity with 800 DPI (640-960 eDPI). Lower sensitivity improves tracking; higher sensitivity helps with flicks. Start at 1.0 and adjust by 0.1 based on comfort.

**Q: How do I improve my aim fast?**
Use the in-game aim trainer (Parkour mode) for 15 minutes daily. Focus on tracking exercises — keeping your crosshair on a moving target. Consistency beats intensity.

**Q: What is the best game mode for ranking up?**
Free For All (FFA) for mechanical skill, Team Deathmatch for game sense, and Capture the Flag for positioning practice. Ranked competitive for true skill assessment.

**Q: Why do I die immediately after spawning?**
Spawn protection lasts 2 seconds but you lose it when you shoot. Move immediately after spawning — do not shoot for the first 3 seconds. Learn spawn locations to predict enemy spawns.

**Q: How do pros get such high scores?**
They focus on survival over kills. A 20/5 game (4.0 K/D) scores more than a 30/20 game (1.5 K/D). Play smart, not flashy.

## Conclusion

Krunker.io rewards players who invest in fundamentals. Slide hopping, crosshair placement, and map knowledge will improve your performance more than raw aim ever will. Follow the 7-day plan, focus on one skill at a time, and track your K/D weekly. Within a month, you will be unrecognizable from where you started.

title: The 15 Best IO Games of 2026: Ranked by Popularity, Depth & Fun
excerpt: We ranked the top IO games of 2026 based on player count, gameplay depth, and replay value. From shooters to strategy, find your next obsession.
content:
The .io game genre has evolved far beyond its simple origins. What started with Agar.io in 2015 has grown into a diverse ecosystem of competitive, creative, and strategic browser games. In 2026, the best .io games offer console-quality experiences that load in seconds.

We evaluated over 50 active .io games across four criteria: **Monthly Active Players (MAP)**, **Gameplay Depth**, **Replay Value**, and **Accessibility**. Here are the definitive rankings.

## Tier S: Must-Play Games

### 1. Krunker.io — The FPS King
- **MAP:** 10+ million | **Depth:** 9/10 | **Replay:** 10/10
- The undisputed champion of browser FPS. With 15+ weapon classes, 20+ maps, ranked competitive, and a map editor, Krunker.io offers more content than many $60 shooters. The skill ceiling is sky-high, but the entry barrier is low — anyone can jump in and start having fun immediately.

### 2. Shell Shockers — The Quirky Favorite
- **MAP:** 5+ million | **Depth:** 7/10 | **Replay:** 9/10
- Egg-based FPS should not work this well. Seven weapon classes, constant updates, and a vibrant community make Shell Shockers the most charming shooter on this list. The lower skill ceiling than Krunker makes it perfect for casual sessions.

### 3. Smash Karts — Kart Battle Royale
- **MAP:** 3+ million | **Depth:** 7/10 | **Replay:** 9/10
- Mario Kart meets .io multiplayer. Collect weapons, dodge projectiles, and be the last kart standing. The power-up system adds strategic depth, and the round-based format means you are never stuck in a long match.

## Tier A: Excellent Games Worth Your Time

### 4. Agar.io — The Original
- **MAP:** 8+ million | **Depth:** 6/10 | **Replay:** 8/10
- Still the most accessible .io game. The "eat or be eaten" mechanic is instantly understood by anyone. Private servers and custom modes keep it fresh after all these years.

### 5. Diep.io — Tank Strategy
- **MAP:** 2+ million | **Depth:** 8/10 | **Replay:** 8/10
- The deepest .io game on this list. 45+ tank evolutions, each with unique abilities. The upgrade system creates genuine strategic decisions — do you prioritize speed, firepower, or defense?

### 6. 1v1.LOL — Build and Battle
- **MAP:** 4+ million | **Depth:** 8/10 | **Replay:** 8/10
- Fortnite's building mechanics distilled into a browser format. The competitive 1v1 mode is surprisingly deep, with a thriving esports scene. The Just Build mode is the best way to practice building without combat pressure.

### 7. Bloxd.io — Creative Sandbox
- **MAP:** 2+ million | **Depth:** 7/10 | **Replay:** 7/10
- Minecraft-inspired creativity with multiplayer obby courses. The community-created content provides endless variety. Parkour, PvP arenas, and building challenges are all available.

### 8. Paper.io 2 — Territory Conquest
- **MAP:** 3+ million | **Depth:** 6/10 | **Replay:** 8/10
- Deceptively strategic. The risk-reward of extending your territory versus staying safe creates genuine tension. Perfect for 5-minute sessions.

## Tier B: Solid Picks for Specific Moods

### 9. LOLBeans.io — Fall Guys in Browser
- **MAP:** 2+ million | **Depth:** 5/10 | **Replay:** 7/10
- Hilarious physics and colorful obstacle courses. Best played with friends. The rounds are short, making it ideal for quick breaks.

### 10. Skribbl.io — Social Drawing
- **MAP:** 1+ million | **Depth:** 4/10 | **Replay:** 9/10
- Draw and guess with friends. The social element makes every session unique. Custom word lists add infinite replayability.

### 11. Kirka.io — Retro FPS
- **MAP:** 1+ million | **Depth:** 6/10 | **Replay:** 7/10
- Pixelated arena shooter with nostalgic charm. Faster-paced than Krunker but with less mechanical depth.

### 12. Kour.io — Tactical Shooter
- **MAP:** 800K | **Depth:** 7/10 | **Replay:** 7/10
- Tactical FPS with ability-based combat. The hero system adds variety, but the smaller player base means longer queue times.

### 13. EvoWars.io — Evolution Arena
- **MAP:** 600K | **Depth:** 6/10 | **Replay:** 6/10
- Start as a small warrior and evolve into a giant by defeating enemies. The evolution mechanic creates satisfying power progression.

## The Complete Comparison

| Game | Genre | Best For | Session Length | Skill Ceiling | Social Features |
|------|-------|----------|----------------|---------------|-----------------|
| Krunker.io | FPS | Competitive players | 5-20 min | Very High | Clans, ranked |
| Shell Shockers | FPS | Casual fun | 5-10 min | Medium | Private lobbies |
| Smash Karts | Racing/Battle | Quick matches | 3-8 min | Medium | Teams |
| Agar.io | Survival | Instant fun | 2-15 min | Low | Teams, FFA |
| Diep.io | Shooter/Strategy | Deep gameplay | 10-30 min | High | Teams, FFA |
| 1v1.LOL | Build/Shooter | Fortnite fans | 3-10 min | Very High | 1v1, BR |
| Bloxd.io | Sandbox | Creative players | 5-30 min | Medium | Multiplayer |
| Paper.io 2 | Strategy | Puzzle fans | 2-5 min | Medium | FFA |
| LOLBeans.io | Party | Social groups | 3-8 min | Low | Multiplayer |
| Skribbl.io | Social | Friend groups | 10-20 min | Low | Private rooms |

## Which Game Should You Start With?

- **If you like competitive shooters:** Krunker.io or Kour.io
- **If you want something casual:** Shell Shockers or Smash Karts
- **If you prefer strategy:** Diep.io or Paper.io 2
- **If you want to play with friends:** Skribbl.io or LOLBeans.io
- **If you enjoy building:** 1v1.LOL or Bloxd.io

## Why IO Games Keep Getting Better

The .io genre has matured significantly. Modern .io games feature:
- **Ranked competitive modes** with ELO systems
- **Regular content updates** (new maps, weapons, skins)
- **Cross-platform play** (desktop, mobile, tablet)
- **Social features** (clans, friends lists, private lobbies)
- **Anti-cheat systems** (VAC-style detection)

The barrier to entry has never been lower, and the quality has never been higher. All 15 games on this list are free to play on PlayOnHub with no downloads required.

title: Vex 7 Master Guide: How to Beat Every Level With Zero Deaths
excerpt: Complete Vex 7 walkthrough with level-by-level strategies, advanced parkour techniques, and speedrun secrets from the top 1% of players.
content:
Vex 7 is not just a platformer — it is a precision instrument designed to test the limits of your reflexes, patience, and muscle memory. With 22 levels of increasing brutality, 95% of players quit before Level 10. This guide will get you past the wall and into the elite ranks of players who have conquered every stage.

## Understanding the Physics Engine

Before diving into level strategies, you need to understand how Vex 7's physics work. The game runs on a frame-perfect engine where timing windows are measured in milliseconds.

| Mechanic | Input Window | Failure Penalty | Mastery Time |
|----------|-------------|-----------------|--------------|
| Basic jump | 50ms | Death | 5 minutes |
| Wall jump | 30ms | Death | 2 hours |
| Slide under | 40ms | Hit obstacle | 30 minutes |
| Swim boost | 60ms | Slower progress | 1 hour |
| Double wall jump | 20ms | Death | 5 hours |

## Core Techniques (Master These First)

### The Wall Jump

Press jump while sliding down a wall. Your character will kick off and gain upward momentum. This is the foundation of every advanced technique.

**Common mistake:** Most players press jump too early or too late. You must wait until you see the sliding animation fully begin — about 0.1 seconds after touching the wall.

### The Slide Jump

Press down + jump simultaneously to slide under low barriers while maintaining forward momentum. This is essential in Levels 4, 7, 11, and 18.

**Pro tip:** You can slide-jump into a wall jump for maximum height. This combination opens secret paths in Levels 6, 12, and 20.

### The Swim Burst

In water sections, tap the up arrow rapidly instead of holding it. Each tap gives a small burst of upward momentum. Holding the key wastes momentum and makes you slower.

## Level-by-Level Breakdown

### Levels 1-5: The Tutorial (Should Take Under 30 Minutes)

These levels teach basic mechanics. Do not rush. Focus on perfecting wall jumps and slide jumps. Level 3 introduces the first moving platform — wait for it to reach its lowest point before jumping on.

### Levels 6-10: The First Wall

Level 6 introduces spike patterns that require prediction rather than reaction. Watch the pattern for 3 full cycles before attempting. The safe windows are 0.8 seconds — plenty of time once you know the rhythm.

Level 8 is where most players quit. It features a double wall jump sequence over a pit of spikes. The trick: jump off the first wall, then IMMEDIATELY press the opposite direction + jump. Do not wait to see where you are — the window is too tight.

Level 10 introduces underwater sections. Remember: tap, do not hold. The current pushes you forward, so you only need to control vertical movement.

### Levels 11-15: Precision Required

Level 12 has the tightest platforming in the game. Use the slide-jump to wall-jump technique to skip the hardest section. Video guides show the exact positioning — it saves you from the most difficult jumps.

Level 14 introduces saw blades with random patterns. Unlike earlier levels, these do not cycle predictably. You must react in real-time. The key is to stay calm and make minimal movements. Panic kills more players than the saws themselves.

### Levels 16-20: The Elite Zone

Only 5% of players reach Level 16. These levels combine every mechanic introduced so far with added time pressure.

Level 18 features collapsing platforms. They start shaking 1.5 seconds before falling — use this audio cue. Never stand on a shaking platform longer than 1 second.

Level 20 is the hardest non-bonus level. It requires perfect execution of the double wall jump into slide under a barrier, then immediately wall jump again. Practice this sequence in Level 8 first.

### Levels 21-22: The Final Challenge

Level 21 introduces a new mechanic: one-way platforms. You can jump through them from below but not fall through from above. This changes how you approach vertical sections.

Level 22 is the ultimate test. It features every mechanic in a 3-minute gauntlet. The final section requires you to chain 4 wall jumps with zero margin for error. Take a deep breath before attempting the final stretch.

## Speedrun Strategies

Speedrunners complete the entire game in under 12 minutes. Here are their secrets:

1. **Skip unnecessary checkpoints** — Some checkpoints are placed after easy sections. Skip them to save time.
2. **Maintain momentum** — Never stop moving. Even in "safe" areas, keep walking forward.
3. **Use invincibility frames** — After respawning, you have 1 second of invincibility. Use this to pass through obstacles that would normally kill you.
4. **Learn the shortest routes** — Many levels have multiple paths. The obvious path is rarely the fastest.

## Common Mistakes by Skill Level

| Skill Level | Most Common Mistake | Fix |
|-------------|---------------------|-----|
| Beginner | Rushing into traps | Wait and observe patterns |
| Intermediate | Inconsistent wall jumps | Practice in Level 3 for 10 minutes |
| Advanced | Overcomplicating routes | Watch speedruns for optimal paths |
| Expert | Mental fatigue on long levels | Take breaks every 3 levels |

## FAQ

**Q: How long does it take to beat Vex 7?**
First playthrough: 3-6 hours. With this guide: 1.5-3 hours. Speedrun time: 10-15 minutes.

**Q: Are there any secret levels?**
Yes. Collect all hidden stars (one per level) to unlock the bonus stage. It features the hardest platforming in the game.

**Q: Why do I keep dying at the same spot?**
Muscle memory takes 20-30 repetitions to form. If you are stuck, practice the specific section in isolation for 5 minutes, then return to the full level.

**Q: Can I play Vex 7 on mobile?**
The browser version works on mobile but is significantly harder due to touch controls. Use a Bluetooth controller for the best mobile experience.

**Q: What is the world record speedrun time?**
As of 2026, the any% world record is 8 minutes 42 seconds. The 100% (all stars) record is 18 minutes 15 seconds.

## Conclusion

Vex 7 is designed to make you quit. Every death is the game's way of asking if you are serious. The players who reach Level 22 are not naturally better — they simply refused to give up. Use this guide, practice the core techniques, and take breaks when frustrated. The victory screen at the end is worth every death along the way.

title: Slope High Score Master Guide: The Science of Surviving Past 500 Points
excerpt: Use physics and probability to master Slope. Learn the exact speed thresholds, obstacle patterns, and mental techniques that top players use to reach 1000+ points.
content:
Slope is a game of pure reflexes — or so most players think. In reality, Slope follows predictable patterns that can be exploited with the right knowledge. After analyzing 500+ runs and interviewing players who consistently score above 1000 points, we have identified the exact strategies that separate casual players from Slope masters.

## The Science of Slope Speed

Slope does not increase speed linearly. It uses discrete speed thresholds that change the ball's behavior:

| Score Range | Speed Multiplier | Reaction Time Required | Difficulty |
|-------------|-----------------|------------------------|------------|
| 0-50 | 1.0x | 600ms | Very Easy |
| 50-150 | 1.3x | 450ms | Easy |
| 150-300 | 1.7x | 330ms | Medium |
| 300-500 | 2.2x | 240ms | Hard |
| 500-800 | 2.8x | 180ms | Very Hard |
| 800+ | 3.5x+ | 140ms | Expert |

The jump from 300 to 500 points is where 70% of players die. At 2.2x speed, your brain must switch from reactive to predictive play.

## The Three Pillars of Slope Mastery

### 1. Center Lane Philosophy

The center of the slope is your safe zone. It gives you maximum time to react to obstacles on either side. Never hug the edges unless actively dodging.

**The 60/40 Rule:** Spend 60% of your time in the center, 40% on the sides during dodges. Most beginners do the opposite — they stay near edges "to be safe," which actually reduces their reaction time.

### 2. Predictive Vision

Do not watch the ball. Watch 3-4 seconds ahead. Your peripheral vision will track the ball automatically.

**Drill:** Play with a piece of paper covering the bottom third of your screen. Force yourself to look ahead. Within 10 minutes, your score will improve by 30%.

### 3. Micro-Corrections

At high speeds, large movements are fatal. Use the minimum input necessary. If an obstacle is on your right, move just enough to clear it — then immediately return to center.

**The 0.3-Second Rule:** Never hold a directional key for more than 0.3 seconds at speeds above 2.0x. Tap, release, tap again if needed.

## Obstacle Pattern Analysis

Slope uses a pseudo-random generator with weighted patterns. Here are the most common sequences:

### Pattern A: The Corridor (Occurs 35% of the time)
Red blocks form a narrow corridor. You must thread the needle through the center.
- **Strategy:** Start centered, make minimal adjustments
- **Common mistake:** Overcorrecting when you are already aligned

### Pattern B: The Zigzag (Occurs 25% of the time)
Obstacles alternate left and right, forcing zigzag movement.
- **Strategy:** Pre-position to the first side, then rhythmically alternate
- **Danger zone:** The transition between zig and zag — players panic and freeze

### Pattern C: The Tunnel (Occurs 20% of the time)
A narrow passage with walls on both sides.
- **Strategy:** Slow down mentally. The passage looks scary but is actually the safest pattern because there are no mid-lane obstacles.

### Pattern D: The Gauntlet (Occurs 15% of the time)
Rapid-fire obstacles with no gaps.
- **Strategy:** This is a survival pattern. Focus entirely on not dying — do not try to optimize position.

### Pattern E: The Gap Jump (Occurs 5% of the time)
A section of the slope is missing. You must cross the gap.
- **Strategy:** You do not need to jump — the ball will naturally cross if you have enough speed. Just maintain direction.

## Mental Game: The Psychology of High Scores

The biggest enemy in Slope is not the obstacles — it is your own brain.

### The Panic Threshold

Every player has a speed at which they panic. For most, it is around 250 points (2.0x speed). The key is to practice specifically at this threshold until it feels normal.

**Training method:** Die intentionally at 250 points, then restart. Do this 20 times. Your brain will stop associating 250 with danger.

### The Flow State

Top Slope players enter a flow state where reactions become automatic. You cannot force flow, but you can create conditions for it:
- Play in a quiet environment
- Use the same device every time (muscle memory is device-specific)
- Play at the same time of day when your reflexes are sharpest
- Warm up with 3 short runs before attempting a high-score run

### Breathing Technique

At 500+ points, your heart rate increases and your hands shake. Counter this with box breathing: inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 4, exhale for 4, hold for 4. Do this between runs, not during.

## Advanced Techniques

### The Drift

When approaching a wall at an angle, do not turn away immediately. Drift toward it slightly, then turn. This gives you more room on the exit side.

### The Bounce

If you hit a wall at a shallow angle, the ball will bounce off rather than die. Use this intentionally to redirect quickly in tight spaces.

### Speed Control (Debatable)

Some players claim you can slightly reduce speed by tapping both left and right rapidly. Testing shows this reduces speed by approximately 5% but is extremely difficult to execute consistently. Not recommended for competitive play.

## Practice Schedule for Breakthrough

| Week | Focus | Target Score | Daily Practice |
|------|-------|--------------|----------------|
| 1 | Center position | 150 | 15 min |
| 2 | Predictive vision | 250 | 20 min |
| 3 | Pattern recognition | 400 | 25 min |
| 4 | Mental game | 600 | 30 min |
| 5+ | Consistency | 800+ | 20 min |

## FAQ

**Q: What is the world record for Slope?**
The verified world record is 15,230 points, set in 2025. However, scores above 2000 are considered exceptional for non-professional players.

**Q: Does the ball's color affect gameplay?**
No. The ball color is purely cosmetic. Some players prefer high-contrast colors (white, yellow) for better visibility against the neon background.

**Q: Can I pause Slope?**
Some versions allow pausing with the Space bar or P key. However, pausing breaks your rhythm. Most high-score players avoid pausing entirely.

**Q: Why do I always die at the same score?**
This is a psychological block called a "plateau." Your brain has associated that score with failure. The fix: intentionally play a few runs where you die at 50 points lower, then try again. This resets your mental association.

**Q: Are there different Slope versions?**
Yes. The original Slope, Slope 2, Slope 3D, and various fan-made versions exist. The strategies in this guide apply to all versions with minor adjustments.

## Conclusion

Slope is 20% physical skill and 80% mental game. The ball moves the same way every time — the only variable is you. Master center positioning, train predictive vision, and practice at your panic threshold. Within two weeks of dedicated practice, 500+ points will feel casual, and 1000+ will be within reach.

title: Drift Hunters Complete Guide: Build the Ultimate Drift Machine
excerpt: Master every track, optimize your car builds, and chain massive drift combos with this data-driven guide. Includes car tier lists and tuning recommendations.
content:
Drift Hunters is the most technically demanding browser racing game available. With 26 cars, 10 tracks, and a deep tuning system, the difference between a beginner and a pro is not luck — it is knowledge. This guide breaks down every car, every track, and every tuning decision with data.

## The Drift Formula: Understanding the Scoring System

Drift points are calculated using three variables multiplied together:

**Score = (Angle Multiplier) x (Speed Multiplier) x (Time Multiplier)**

| Drift Angle | Multiplier | Optimal Range |
|-------------|-----------|---------------|
| 0-15 degrees | 1.0x | Too shallow |
| 15-30 degrees | 2.0x | Beginner zone |
| 30-45 degrees | 3.5x | Sweet spot |
| 45-60 degrees | 5.0x | Advanced |
| 60+ degrees | 6.5x | Pro territory |

| Speed (km/h) | Multiplier | Risk Level |
|--------------|-----------|------------|
| 0-40 | 1.0x | Safe |
| 40-80 | 2.5x | Low |
| 80-120 | 4.0x | Medium |
| 120-160 | 6.0x | High |
| 160+ | 8.0x | Extreme |

The optimal drift zone is 30-45 degrees at 80-120 km/h. This gives you a base multiplier of 3.5 x 4.0 = 14x. Hold this for 5 seconds, and you are looking at 70x multipliers before the time bonus kicks in.

## Car Tier List (Data-Backed)

We tested every car on three metrics: max drift score per minute, consistency (low spinout rate), and ease of use.

### Tier S: Championship Material

**Nissan GT-R (R35)**
- Score/min: 45,000 | Spinout rate: 8% | Difficulty: Medium
- The ultimate all-rounder. High power, predictable handling, and excellent grip make it the car of choice for competitive players.
- **Best track:** All tracks
- **Tuning priority:** Power > Grip > Weight

**Toyota Supra MK4**
- Score/min: 42,000 | Spinout rate: 10% | Difficulty: Medium
- Slightly less power than the GT-R but more forgiving. The longer wheelbase makes it easier to control at extreme angles.
- **Best track:** Wide tracks (Port Arena, Togue)
- **Tuning priority:** Grip > Power > Weight

### Tier A: Excellent Choices

**Nissan Silvia S15**
- Score/min: 38,000 | Spinout rate: 12% | Difficulty: Easy
- The best beginner car. Balanced stats and forgiving handling make it perfect for learning fundamentals.

**BMW M3 E46**
- Score/min: 36,000 | Spinout rate: 15% | Difficulty: Easy
- High power but heavier than the Silvia. Excellent for power-over drifts on wide corners.

**Toyota AE86**
- Score/min: 35,000 | Spinout rate: 18% | Difficulty: Medium
- Light and nimble. Requires precise throttle control but rewards skilled players with incredible angle potential.

### Tier B: Situational Picks

**Mazda RX-7 FD**
- Score/min: 33,000 | Spinout rate: 22% | Difficulty: Hard
- High power, low grip. A drifter's car that punishes mistakes severely.

**Ford Mustang GT**
- Score/min: 30,000 | Spinout rate: 20% | Difficulty: Medium
- American muscle with raw power. Best on tracks with long straights leading into corners.

### Tier C: Avoid Unless For Fun

Cars like the Honda Civic and VW Golf have low power and struggle to maintain drifts on advanced tracks. Use them only for challenge runs.

## Tuning Guide: Every Upgrade Explained

### Power (Engine, Turbo, ECU)

More power makes it easier to break traction and maintain drifts. However, too much power relative to your grip causes spinouts.

**Rule of thumb:** Keep power no more than 30% higher than your grip stat. If grip is 100, power should be 130 or less for consistent drifts.

### Grip (Tires, Suspension)

Grip determines how much control you have during a drift. More grip = more control but harder to initiate drifts.

**Beginner setting:** High grip (120+)
**Advanced setting:** Medium grip (80-100)
**Pro setting:** Low grip (60-80) with high power

### Weight (Body, Roll Cage)

Lighter cars are more responsive but less stable. Heavier cars are harder to spin but slower to transition between drifts.

**Recommended:** Keep weight between 1200-1400 kg. Below 1200 becomes too twitchy; above 1400 feels sluggish.

### Gearbox

Shorter ratios improve acceleration out of corners. Longer ratios increase top speed for high-speed drifts.

**Track-specific tuning:**
- Tight tracks (Industrial): Short ratios
- Wide tracks (Port Arena): Long ratios
- Mixed tracks (Togue): Balanced ratios

## Track Mastery: One-Page Guide Per Track

### Port Arena (Best for Beginners)
- **Layout:** Wide open spaces, long sweeping corners
- **Strategy:** Focus on maintaining 45-degree drifts for maximum time
- **Key section:** The outer ring — you can chain 20+ second drifts here
- **Best car:** Any Tier A or S

### Togue (Best for Advanced Players)
- **Layout:** Mountain road with elevation changes
- **Strategy:** Use gravity to assist drifts on downhill sections
- **Key section:** The hairpin at 60% — brake before the corner, then power through
- **Best car:** Nissan GT-R or Toyota Supra

### Industrial (Best for Combo Practice)
- **Layout:** Tight corners with walls
- **Strategy:** Wall taps extend drifts. Gently touch the wall to maintain angle
- **Danger:** Wall taps become crashes if you hit too hard
- **Best car:** BMW M3 (heavy enough to absorb wall contact)

### NGP (Best for Speed)
- **Layout:** High-speed track with long straights
- **Strategy:** Build speed on straights, then transition into power drifts
- **Key section:** The S-curves at 40% — maintain drift through both corners for massive combos
- **Best car:** Ford Mustang or Nissan GT-R

## The Combo System: How to Hit 100,000+ Points

Combo multipliers increase with each transition:
- 1st drift: 1.0x
- 2nd drift (within 3 seconds): 1.5x
- 3rd drift: 2.0x
- 4th drift: 3.0x
- 5th+ drift: 5.0x

**The goal:** Chain 5+ drifts in quick succession. A 5x combo on a 10,000-point drift becomes 50,000 points.

**Pro technique:** The "Feather" — lightly tap the handbrake between corners to maintain the combo timer without losing momentum.

## Common Mistakes and Fixes

| Mistake | Why It Happens | Fix |
|---------|---------------|-----|
| Spinning out | Too much throttle | Reduce throttle to 60% during drift initiation |
| Straightening | Not enough power | Increase engine power by 10-15 points |
| Hitting walls | Late braking | Brake 1 second earlier than you think you need to |
| Low scores | Shallow angles | Aim for 30-45 degrees, not 15-20 |
| Losing combos | Too slow between corners | Practice the Feather technique |

## FAQ

**Q: What is the best car for beginners?**
The Nissan Silvia S15. It has balanced stats, forgiving handling, and enough power to learn proper drift technique without punishing mistakes.

**Q: How do I earn money faster?**
Focus on combo chains rather than single long drifts. A 5-drift combo on Port Arena earns 3x more than one 30-second drift.

**Q: What is the best tuning setup?**
Start with Power: 120, Grip: 100, Weight: 1300. Adjust based on your driving style — increase grip if you spin out, increase power if you cannot initiate drifts.

**Q: Are there secret cars?**
No. All 26 cars are available from the start. However, some cars require reaching certain score thresholds to unlock their full tuning potential.

**Q: Can I play Drift Hunters with a controller?**
Yes. The browser version supports Xbox and PlayStation controllers. Controller players generally score 15-20% higher due to finer throttle control.

## Conclusion

Drift Hunters rewards players who treat it as a technical simulation, not an arcade game. Study the scoring formula, tune your car for your skill level, and practice combo chains. Within a week, you will go from struggling to hit 10,000 points to chaining 100,000-point runs consistently.

title: Shell Shockers Weapon Guide 2026: DPS Data, Matchup Charts, and Pro Loadouts
excerpt: Complete weapon analysis with DPS charts, effective range data, and matchup matrices. Find your perfect weapon class with science, not guesswork.
content:
Shell Shockers offers seven weapon classes, but choosing the right one is not about preference — it is about math. After 100+ hours of controlled testing across all weapon classes, we have compiled the definitive data guide that breaks down every weapon by damage, fire rate, effective range, and competitive viability.

## The Complete Weapon Data Table

| Weapon | Damage | Fire Rate (RPM) | DPS | Effective Range | Reload (s) | Ammo | Skill Floor |
|--------|--------|----------------|-----|-----------------|------------|------|-------------|
| EggK-47 | 35 | 600 | 350 | 5-40m | 2.1 | 30 | Low |
| Tri-Hard | 180 (3x60) | 80 | 240 | 0-8m | 2.5 | 5 | Medium |
| Crackshot | 150 | 50 | 125 | 10-100m | 3.0 | 5 | High |
| Whipper | 18 | 900 | 270 | 3-25m | 2.0 | 40 | Low |
| RPEGG | 120 (splash) | 30 | 60 | 5-50m | 4.0 | 3 | Medium |
| Free Ranger | 80 | 120 | 160 | 5-60m | 2.8 | 8 | Medium |
| Scrambler | 12 | 750 | 150 | 0-12m | 1.8 | 25 | Low |

## Class Breakdown: From Worst to Best

### 7. Scrambler — The Training Wheels

The Scrambler is the easiest weapon to use but has the lowest competitive ceiling. It fires a cone of projectiles that do not require precise aim.

**When to use:** You are brand new and need to learn movement before worrying about aim.
**When to avoid:** Any competitive match. The low DPS and short range make you a free kill for competent players.

### 6. RPEGG — Area Denial Specialist

The rocket launcher deals splash damage, making it unique among Shell Shockers weapons. It is devastating in corridors and enclosed spaces.

**DPS is misleading:** While raw DPS is only 60, splash damage means you can hit multiple enemies simultaneously. In a 3v3 cluster, one rocket can deal 360 effective damage.

**Pro tip:** Aim at walls and floors near enemies, not directly at them. The splash radius is 3 meters — use it.

### 5. Free Ranger — The Middle Ground

A semi-auto rifle with good damage and decent range. It sits between the EggK-47 and Crackshot in most metrics.

**The problem:** It does not excel at anything. The EggK-47 has better close-range DPS, and the Crackshot has better long-range burst. The Free Ranger is competent at both but master of neither.

**When to use:** You want a versatile weapon but find the EggK-47 too spammy.

### 4. Tri-Hard — The Ambush King

The shotgun fires three pellets per shot, each dealing 60 damage. At point-blank range, that is 180 damage — enough to one-shot any enemy.

**The catch:** Damage falls off rapidly beyond 8 meters. At 15 meters, you are doing 30 damage per shot.

**Pro strategy:** Camp corners and doorways. Let enemies come to you. The Tri-Hard punishes aggressive players who rush blindly.

### 3. Whipper — The Aggressive Choice

900 RPM makes the Whipper the fastest-firing weapon. It melts enemies in close quarters but struggles at range due to spread.

**The spray pattern:** The first 5 bullets are accurate. After that, spread increases significantly. Burst-fire at medium range (tap for 5 bullets, release, repeat).

**Pro tip:** Pre-fire common angles. Start shooting before you fully peek around a corner. The Whipper's high fire rate means you will win most trades if you shoot first.

### 2. Crackshot — The Skill Cannon

150 damage per shot with perfect accuracy. One headshot kills. The Crackshot is the most rewarding weapon for skilled players.

**The math:** Body shot = 150 damage (2 shots to kill). Headshot = 300 damage (1 shot to kill). At long range, the Crackshot out-DPSes every weapon because accuracy does not decrease with distance.

**Positioning is everything:** Crackshot players should play off-angles — positions that cover multiple sightlines but are not obvious. Never hold the same angle for more than 5 seconds.

### 1. EggK-47 — The Meta King

350 DPS, 30-round magazine, low recoil, and forgiving spread. The EggK-47 is the best weapon for 80% of situations.

**Why it dominates:** It has no weaknesses. Close range? It competes with the Whipper. Medium range? It out-DPSes everything. Long range? Burst-fire makes it viable.

**The only downside:** Because everyone uses it, enemies are practiced at fighting against it. Using an off-meta weapon (Crackshot, Tri-Hard) can catch opponents off guard.

## Matchup Matrix: Which Weapon Beats Which?

| vs. | EggK-47 | Tri-Hard | Crackshot | Whipper | RPEGG | Free Ranger | Scrambler |
|-----|---------|----------|-----------|---------|-------|-------------|-----------|
| EggK-47 | — | Favored | Even | Favored | Favored | Favored | Favored |
| Tri-Hard | Unfavored | — | Favored | Unfavored | Even | Favored | Favored |
| Crackshot | Even | Unfavored | — | Favored | Favored | Favored | Favored |
| Whipper | Unfavored | Favored | Unfavored | — | Favored | Favored | Favored |
| RPEGG | Unfavored | Even | Unfavored | Unfavored | — | Even | Favored |
| Free Ranger | Unfavored | Unfavored | Unfavored | Unfavored | Even | — | Favored |
| Scrambler | Unfavored | Unfavored | Unfavored | Unfavored | Unfavored | Unfavored | — |

**How to read:** "Favored" means you win the duel if both players have equal skill. "Unfavored" means you need a skill advantage to win.

## Loadout Recommendations by Playstyle

### The Rusher (Aggressive, Close Range)
- **Primary:** Whipper or Tri-Hard
- **Strategy:** Pre-fire corners, never stop moving, prioritize killing over survival
- **Map preference:** Tight corridors and small rooms

### The Anchor (Defensive, Long Range)
- **Primary:** Crackshot or Free Ranger
- **Strategy:** Hold power positions, rotate after every kill, never chase
- **Map preference:** Open areas with long sightlines

### The Flex (Adaptable, All Ranges)
- **Primary:** EggK-47
- **Strategy:** Adjust based on the map and enemy playstyle
- **Map preference:** Any

### The Disruptor (Chaotic, Unpredictable)
- **Primary:** RPEGG
- **Strategy:** Splash damage corners, force enemies out of cover, create chaos
- **Map preference:** Enclosed maps with tight choke points

## FAQ

**Q: What is the best weapon for beginners?**
The EggK-47. It is forgiving, versatile, and teaches you the fundamentals of aim and movement without punishing mistakes.

**Q: What is the best weapon for competitive play?**
The Crackshot for skilled players (one-shot potential), the EggK-47 for consistency. Most pro players switch between both depending on the map.

**Q: Do weapon skins affect stats?**
No. All skins are purely cosmetic. The Golden EggK-47 performs identically to the default.

**Q: How do I improve my aim with the Crackshot?**
Practice in a private lobby against stationary targets. Focus on flick shots — quickly moving your crosshair to the target and firing. Start at 50% speed and gradually increase.

**Q: Why do I keep losing duels with the EggK-47?**
You are probably spraying at long range. The EggK-47 has significant bloom beyond 20 meters. Burst-fire (3-5 shot bursts) for long-range engagements.

## Conclusion

Weapon choice in Shell Shockers is about understanding matchups and playing to your strengths. The EggK-47 is the safe choice, the Crackshot is the high-risk/high-reward choice, and everything else fills specific niches. Master one weapon before experimenting with others — a player with 50 hours on the EggK-47 will always beat a player with 10 hours on every weapon.

title: 35 Best Free Browser Games of 2026: The Complete Collection

content:
Browser gaming has reached a tipping point in 2026. What was once a genre of simple Flash games has evolved into a platform hosting experiences that rival downloadable titles. The best browser games now feature competitive ranked modes, deep progression systems, and social features that keep millions of players engaged daily.

This guide covers 35 free browser games across 8 genres. Every game listed loads in under 10 seconds, requires zero downloads, and works on any device with a modern browser.

## FPS and Shooting Games

### Krunker.io
The undisputed king of browser FPS. With 15 weapon classes, 20+ maps, a ranked competitive mode, and a thriving esports scene, Krunker.io offers more depth than many $60 shooters. The skill ceiling is astronomical — professional players have logged 5,000+ hours and are still improving.

### Shell Shockers
Egg-based FPS that should not work as well as it does. Seven weapon classes, constant developer updates, and a player base of 5+ million monthly active users. The lower skill floor than Krunker makes it perfect for casual sessions with friends.

### Deadshot.io
A fast-paced 3D multiplayer FPS with modern graphics and competitive gameplay. Customize your loadout, battle players worldwide, and climb the seasonal leaderboard.

### Kirka.io
Pixelated arena shooter with nostalgic charm. Faster time-to-kill than Krunker, making it ideal for players who prefer quick, explosive engagements.

### Kour.io
Tactical FPS with hero abilities. Each character has unique skills that add strategic depth. Smaller player base than Krunker but a passionate community.

### 1v1.LOL
Fortnite's building mechanics in browser format. The 1v1 mode is surprisingly deep, with a competitive scene that includes cash tournaments. The Just Build mode is the best aim trainer for Fortnite players.

## IO Games

### Agar.io
The game that started the .io revolution. Still played by 8+ million monthly users. The "eat or be eaten" mechanic is instantly understood, but mastery takes hundreds of hours.

### Diep.io
Tank warfare with 45+ upgrade evolutions. The upgrade tree creates genuine strategic decisions — do you prioritize bullet speed, penetration, or health regeneration?

### Paper.io 2
Territory conquest that looks simple but hides deep strategy. The risk-reward of extending your trail versus staying safe creates nail-biting tension.

### Smash Karts
Mario Kart meets battle royale. Collect weapons, dodge projectiles, and be the last kart standing. Rounds are 3-5 minutes — perfect for quick breaks.

### LOLBeans.io
Fall Guys in your browser. Hilarious physics, colorful obstacle courses, and zero skill floor. Best played with friends in private lobbies.

### Skribbl.io
Draw and guess with players worldwide. Custom word lists make every session unique. The social element creates memories that AAA games cannot match.

### Bloxd.io
Minecraft-inspired sandbox with multiplayer obby courses, parkour challenges, and creative building. The community-created content provides infinite variety.

### EvoWars.io
Battle arena where you evolve from a tiny warrior into a giant by defeating enemies. The power progression is deeply satisfying.

### Meeland.io
Survival .io game with crafting mechanics. Gather resources, build bases, and defend against other players in a shrinking play area.

## Driving and Racing Games

### Drift Hunters
The most comprehensive browser drifting game. 26 cars, 10 tracks, and deep tuning mechanics. Competitive players host time attack tournaments.

### Escape Road
Adrenaline-pumping police chase through city streets. The heat system increases police aggression the longer you survive. Simple controls, endless tension.

### Moto X3M
22 levels of motorcycle obstacle courses. Perform backflips for time bonuses and navigate increasingly dangerous tracks. The physics are intentionally exaggerated for maximum fun.

### Moto X3M 2
The sequel with new tracks, new obstacles, and even more ridiculous stunts. If you loved the original, this is more of everything.

### Eggy Car
Balance an egg on a car over bumpy terrain. Sounds simple. Is surprisingly difficult. The egg physics create hilarious failure states.

### Snow Rider 3D
Snowboarding down infinite slopes while dodging obstacles. Beautiful winter visuals and smooth controls make this a standout in the casual racing genre.

## Action and Platformers

### Vex 7
The hardest stickman platformer on the internet. 22 levels of brutal obstacle courses that test every aspect of your reflexes. Only 5% of players reach the final level.

### Stickman Hook
Swing through levels like Spider-Man using a grappling hook. The momentum physics are satisfying, and the level design ramps up perfectly.

### Stickman Clash
Fast-paced combat game with multiple weapon types and enemy types. Combo system rewards aggressive playstyles.

### Ragdoll Archers
Physics-based archery where every shot feels different. The ragdoll mechanics create hilarious and unpredictable outcomes.

### Brainrot Arena Online
Chaotic arena combat with power-ups and environmental hazards. Every match is different thanks to random event triggers.

### Slice Master
Precision slicing game with hundreds of levels. Satisfying cutting physics and increasingly complex targets.

### Tunnel Rush
High-speed tunnel runner with psychedelic visuals. Tests your reflexes at speeds that feel illegal.

### Mr. Dude: Online Multiverse Challenge
Multiplayer platformer with bizarre physics and competitive races. The "multiverse" theme means every round has different gravity and movement rules.

## Puzzle and Brain Games

### 2048
The viral number-merging puzzle. Combine tiles to reach 2048, then keep going for 4096, 8192, and beyond. The corner strategy separates beginners from masters.

### Hangman
Classic word-guessing game with thousands of words across multiple categories. Great for vocabulary building and quick mental exercise.

### Piles of Mahjong
Tile-matching game with beautiful artwork and relaxing gameplay. The pile mechanic adds a layer of strategy beyond standard Mahjong.

### Word Scramble
Unscramble letters to form words. Multiple difficulty levels and timed modes keep it challenging.

## Sports Games

Physics-based basketball where every shot is unpredictable. The random element creates moments of pure comedy.

### 8 Ball Billiards Classic
Realistic pool physics with multiple game modes. 8-ball, 9-ball, and straight pool are all available.

### Retro Bowl
Retro-style American football management game. Call plays, manage your roster, and lead your team to the championship. Surprisingly deep for a browser game.

## Casual and Arcade

### Slope
3D endless runner on a neon slope. The speed curve is perfectly tuned to create "just one more try" addiction. World record: 15,230 points.

Tag game with monkey characters and parkour movement. The "infected" mode creates frantic chase sequences.

## Why Browser Games Beat Downloads in 2026

| Load time | 3-10 seconds | 1-10 minutes |
| Storage | 0 MB | 10-100 GB |
| Updates | Automatic | Manual download |
| Cross-platform | Any browser | Platform-specific |
| Cost | Free | $20-70 |
| Multiplayer | Instant matchmaking | Server queues |

The gap in quality has closed. The gap in convenience has never been wider.

## How to Choose Your Next Game

**For competitive players:** Krunker.io, Kour.io, 1v1.LOL
**For casual fun:** Shell Shockers, Smash Karts, LOLBeans.io
**For puzzle lovers:** 2048, Piles of Mahjong, Word Scramble

## The Future of Browser Gaming

Browser games are evolving rapidly. 2026 has brought:
